proposals.name: LLM Direct (GPT-4o 5-shot)
proposals.hypothesis: GPT-4o is the top performer (0.703). Few-shot did not help Sonnet, but GPT-4o may exploit exemplar formatting/calibration better. This tests whether the best base model benefits from in-context examples, isolating the few_shot effect on the leader rather than a mid-pack model.

proposals.name: Stat+LLM Direct (GPT-4o) Hybrid
proposals.hypothesis: Stat features hurt when funneled through a Poisson head (E05-E07 stuck at 0.547), but that may be a model_type bottleneck, not the features. Letting GPT-4o reason directly over stat+LLM features (rather than emitting Poisson rates) may retain its 0.703 reasoning advantage while adding ELO/form/H2H grounding. Critical ablation: does the LLM head or the feature_set drive the stat-LLM degradation?

proposals.name: LLM Direct (o3-mini high reasoning)
proposals.hypothesis: E13 ran o3-mini but scored poorly (0.406); E08 errored on a parameter incompatibility. With reasoning_effort explicitly set to high and the correct API params, o3-mini's chain-of-thought may improve calibration. Tests whether reasoning models are inherently weak at this task or were just misconfigured.

proposals.name: Stat+LLM Poisson (o3-mini) fixed API
proposals.hypothesis: E08 failed only due to the max_tokens vs max_completion_tokens API error, not a modeling issue. Re-running with corrected params completes the Stat+LLM Poisson sweep across all four LLMs, giving a clean comparison point (vs E05-E07 at 0.547) and recovering a lost data point.

proposals.name: LLM Direct Ensemble Inputs (Opus+GPT-4o agreement)
proposals.hypothesis: Opus 0-shot (0.656) trailed GPT-4o. Adding few-shot exemplars to Opus tests whether the strongest reasoning-capable Claude can close the gap with GPT-4o when given calibrated examples, and provides a second few-shot data point alongside E14/E15 to determine if few-shot's null effect generalizes across model families.
